title: Check if a string is a substring of another

content.problemStatement:
Given two strings, `S1` and `S2`, your task is to determine whether `S2` is a substring of `S1`. If `S2` is a substring of `S1`, print "YES". Otherwise, print "NO".

A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within a string. For example, "abc" is a substring of "aabcda", but "ac" is not a contiguous sequence in "aabcda".


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer `T`, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:

The first line contains the string `S1`.
The second line contains the string `S2`.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing either "YES" or "NO", depending on whether `S2` is a substring of `S1`.
constraints: S1 and S2 consist only of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains the string S1, the second line contains the string S2. Both strings contain only lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing "YES" if S2 is a substring of S1, otherwise print "NO".

sampleTestCases.input:
4
hello
ell
codechef
chef
programming
debug
abcd
efgh
sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first case "ell" appears in "hello". In the second case "chef" appears in "codechef". The third and fourth cases have no occurrence, so the answer is NO.
The whole string S2 equals S1, therefore it is a substring.
S2 is longer than S1, so it cannot be a substring.

hidden.label: mixed stress test
hidden.input:
4
codechef
chef
hello
world
aaaaab
aaab
xyz
y

solution.approach: For each test case we need to check whether S2 occurs contiguously inside S1. The standard library functions (strstr in C, string::find in C++, contains in Java and Python, includes in JavaScript) implement this check efficiently, typically using a linear scan (O(|S1|+|S2|)). We simply read the strings and apply the appropriate function. If the function reports a position, we output "YES", otherwise "NO".
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S1|+|S2|) per test case, O(total length of all strings) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input strings.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    T=int(data[0])
    out=[]
    idx=1
    for _ in range(T):
        S1=data[idx].strip()
        S2=data[idx+1].strip()
        idx+=2
        out.append('YES' if S2 in S1 else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
